message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="team-lead" summary="9/9 consensus! Mini-retro then Step 5">
9/9 consensus achieved on Step 4! Well done. 

Per the pipeline, please hold your mini-retro now (1-minute checkpoint) — what went well, what to improve, any process observations. Then the team should decide on Step 5: Clarify as Next Action + Trash, including who should drive.
</teammate-message>

content.text: Good, 9/9 consensus reached. Let me contribute my mini-retro observations to the team.

input.content:
Heydon Pickering -- Mini-retro observations for Step 4:

**What went well:**
- Accessible names on interactive elements (Complete/Delete/Edit with item titles) were correct from the first commit. The team has clearly internalized this pattern.
- The context grouping with `<section>` and `<h2>` headings was a good structural decision -- it gives screen reader users a way to navigate by context using heading shortcuts.
- Live region announcements via `htmx_response_with_announce` continue to work well.
- Forced-colors support was included without being asked.

**What to improve:**
- Focus management and visible labels were missed in the initial implementation despite being in the Definition of Done checklist. These are recurring items -- the same focus management gap existed in the old todo list and was solved there with `todo-focus.js`, so the pattern was available. Suggestion: when building a new page with HTMX item removal, copy/adapt the focus management script as part of the initial implementation, not as a fix-up.
- The visible label requirement ("All form inputs have visible `<label>` elements") was also in the DoD but missed on the first pass. Consider treating the DoD accessibility checklist as a pre-commit gate rather than a review catch.

**Process observation:**
- Two review rounds were needed to get the blocking items addressed. On the first re-review request, the items hadn't actually been fixed yet. Clear communication about which specific items have been addressed in each commit would help reviewers target their re-review.

**For Step 5 (Clarify as Next Action + Trash):**
- The clarify form will need radio buttons for destination choice with progressive disclosure. Accessibility considerations: radio group with `<fieldset>`/`<legend>`, revealed fields properly associated, focus management when fields appear/disappear.
- Trash (delete) is straightforward from an a11y perspective -- we have the pattern.
- I'd recommend Luca or Carson as Driver since this involves both HTMX progressive disclosure and Rust route handling.
input.summary: Mini-retro: focus mgmt pattern should be day-1, not fix-up

routing.content:
Heydon Pickering -- Mini-retro observations for Step 4:

**What went well:**
- Accessible names on interactive elements (Complete/Delete/Edit with item titles) were correct from the first commit. The team has clearly internalized this pattern.
- The context grouping with `<section>` and `<h2>` headings was a good structural decision -- it gives screen reader users a way to navigate by context using heading shortcuts.
- Live region announcements via `htmx_response_with_announce` continue to work well.
- Forced-colors support was included without being asked.

**What to improve:**
- Focus management and visible labels were missed in the initial implementation despite being in the Definition of Done checklist. These are recurring items -- the same focus management gap existed in the old todo list and was solved there with `todo-focus.js`, so the pattern was available. Suggestion: when building a new page with HTMX item removal, copy/adapt the focus management script as part of the initial implementation, not as a fix-up.
- The visible label requirement ("All form inputs have visible `<label>` elements") was also in the DoD but missed on the first pass. Consider treating the DoD accessibility checklist as a pre-commit gate rather than a review catch.

**Process observation:**
- Two review rounds were needed to get the blocking items addressed. On the first re-review request, the items hadn't actually been fixed yet. Clear communication about which specific items have been addressed in each commit would help reviewers target their re-review.

**For Step 5 (Clarify as Next Action + Trash):**
- The clarify form will need radio buttons for destination choice with progressive disclosure. Accessibility considerations: radio group with `<fieldset>`/`<legend>`, revealed fields properly associated, focus management when fields appear/disappear.
- Trash (delete) is straightforward from an a11y perspective -- we have the pattern.
- I'd recommend Luca or Carson as Driver since this involves both HTMX progressive disclosure and Rust route handling.

input.content:
Mini-retro contribution sent to Luca (Driver). Key points:

**Went well:** Accessible names, context grouping with headings, live region announcements, forced-colors support all correct from day one.

**Improve:** Focus management and visible labels were missed on first pass despite being in the DoD. Suggestion: treat the a11y checklist as a pre-commit gate, and copy/adapt focus management scripts (like `todo-focus.js`) when building new pages with HTMX item removal -- don't wait for review to catch it.

**Process:** The first re-review request came before the fixes were actually in place, requiring a second round. Clearer commit-to-fix mapping would help.

**Step 5 input:** The clarify form needs `<fieldset>`/`<legend>` for radio button groups, proper progressive disclosure a11y (revealed fields associated, focus management when fields appear/disappear). I'd support Luca or Carson as Driver.
input.summary: Mini-retro done, ready for Step 5 discussion

content.text:
Mini-retro contribution submitted. I'm ready for Step 5 (Clarify as Next Action + Trash) when the team decides on the Driver and begins work. 

Key a11y considerations I've flagged for Step 5:
- The clarify form with radio buttons needs `<fieldset>`/`<legend>` wrapping
- Progressive disclosure (showing/hiding fields based on radio selection) needs proper association and focus management
- Trash/delete follows existing patterns -- no new a11y concerns there
